_note: COMMUNITY / THIRD-PARTY tool: superagent-ai/grok-cli (3,140 stars; owner is an Org with 467 followers). NOT an official xAI product. The official xAI CLI is 'Grok Build' (separate tool, TOML config). The file-based agent definition here is the subAgents[] array (JSON objects) inside ~/.grok/user-settings.json. Official Grok Build does NOT publish a documented per-subagent file schema (unverified); its subagents are spawned at runtime via plan mode / git worktrees.

paths: .grok/settings.json (project settings; model + mcpServers, no subAgents documented)
agentFile.format: JSON for settings/subAgents; Markdown for GROK.md/AGENTS.md/SKILL.md instruction files
agentFile.bodyIs: For subAgents[]: a JSON object entry (no markdown body). For instruction files (GROK.md/AGENTS.md): free-form Markdown prose treated as system-prompt instructions; no formal frontmatter schema.

name.allowed: Any string EXCEPT reserved names: general, explore, vision, verify, computer
name.description: Subagent identifier; selectable/managed via the /agents command in the TUI.

model.allowed: A valid xAI model id (e.g. grok-4.3, grok-3-fast)
model.description: Model used when this subagent runs; overrides the default model for the subagent's turns.

instruction.description: The subagent's system prompt / persona instruction (e.g. 'Prioritize security implications and suggest concrete fixes.').

_userSettingsContext.allowed: Sibling top-level keys in ~/.grok/user-settings.json: apiKey (string), defaultModel (string), subAgents (array of the above), mcp (object of MCP servers), hooks (object keyed by lifecycle event), telegram (object: botToken, audioInput{enabled,language}), sandbox/resource settings (object). File perms 0o600.
_userSettingsContext.description: subAgents[] lives inside user-settings.json alongside these keys. Project file .grok/settings.json documents only 'model' and 'mcpServers'.

bashInjection.description: No documented in-instruction shell-command substitution token inside GROK.md/AGENTS.md/instruction. (Note: the separate 'hooks' feature can RUN shell commands on lifecycle events, but that is config-driven, not in-content instruction templating.) unverified - no evidence of an inline bash-injection token.

fileInjection.description: No documented inline @file / file-embed token within instruction text. Instead, the system auto-merges AGENTS.md/AGENTS.override.md hierarchically (git root -> cwd) and loads GROK.md global+project; precedence: project over global. This is directory-hierarchy merging, not an in-content injection directive. unverified for any explicit token.

argumentTokens.description: No documented argument/placeholder templating (e.g. $1, $ARGUMENTS) for subAgents or GROK.md/AGENTS.md. unverified.

toolReference.description: subAgents[] entries have only name/model/instruction; no documented per-agent tool allow/deny field, and no in-content directive to restrict or name tools. unverified - no tool-scoping field found.

mcpReference.token: mcpServers (in .grok/settings.json) / mcp (in ~/.grok/user-settings.json); managed via /mcps in TUI
mcpReference.description: MCP servers are declared as JSON config (not an in-instruction token): keys like command, args, env; transports include stdio, HTTP, and SSE. They are registered globally/project-wide, not referenced from within a subagent's instruction text.

variableTemplating.description: No documented variable interpolation in instruction files beyond environment-variable resolution for config values (GROK_API_KEY, GROK_BASE_URL, GROK_MODEL, GROK_MAX_TOKENS). No in-content {{var}}-style templating. unverified.

agentMention.description: No documented in-content @agent mention/delegation token within instruction text; subagents are selected/managed via the /agents TUI command. unverified for any inline mention syntax.

_hooksEventSyntax.scope: user-settings.json (and/or project settings); lifecycle automation, not in-content instruction
_hooksEventSyntax.description: Lifecycle hooks are JSON config: keyed by event (e.g. PreToolUse), each with a 'matcher' (e.g. 'bash') and a 'hooks' array of {type:'command', command:<path/script>, timeout:<seconds>}. Runs external commands around tool use; config-driven, not instruction templating.

_slashCommandsFromSkills.description: User-invocable skills appear as slash commands; SKILL.md is Markdown. Its frontmatter field schema is unverified.

sources.note: Community/3rd-party. Owner 'superagent-ai' is an Organization (467 org followers). Passes >100-star threshold. README: subAgents (name/model/instruction), reserved names, GROK.md/AGENTS.md, hooks, skills, MCP.
Community docs (DeepWiki) for superagent-ai/grok-cli: file locations, JSON field specs, resolution order, AGENTS.md merge behavior, MCP transports.
